problem_statement: Pattern cards contain structured signal_sequence, threshold_estimated, lag, and diagnostic fields that describe exactly what to monitor and when — but engineers must manually translate this into alert configurations after the pipeline passes. This creates a gap between 'knowing the pattern applies' (enforced at MMD) and 'having monitoring in place for it' (currently manual, frequently skipped). The generator closes this gap by producing platform-specific alert config files from card fields at pipeline time.

prior_solutions_considered.approach: Embed alert configs directly in each pattern card JSON
prior_solutions_considered.rejected_because: Tightly couples cards to specific monitoring platforms. Cards would need updating every time a platform's alert schema changes. Cards should describe failure patterns, not monitoring syntax.

prior_solutions_considered.approach: Generate alerts from the full card prose using an LLM
prior_solutions_considered.rejected_because: Introduces non-determinism. The same card produces different outputs on different runs. Alert configs must be stable and diffable — engineers need to see exactly what changed between card versions.

prior_solutions_considered.approach: Require engineers to write alert configs manually and store them alongside cards
prior_solutions_considered.rejected_because: This is exactly the problem we are solving. Manual = skipped.

claims.claim: The signal_sequence[first_anomaly].signal field in each card is specific enough to map to a named metric category (error rate, latency, queue depth, etc.) even if not to an exact PromQL expression
Prometheus alert rule YAML is the right first platform — it is open-standard, widely used, and its schema is stable and well-documented
The lag field ('minutes', 'hours', etc.) can be mapped to a Prometheus 'for:' duration with a reasonable default (minutes → 5m, hours → 30m)
A FILL_IN marker pattern is an accepted practice in infrastructure-as-code templates and will not confuse engineers

known_constraints: signal_sequence fields are prose — cannot derive exact PromQL expressions without per-pattern human annotation
Must not generate alerts from DRAFT cards without a prominent warning
Platform adapters must be pluggable — new platforms added without modifying core generator
Generator must read from live PatternRegistry, not a static snapshot
code_shapes: module that reads structured data and produces formatted file output
template rendering with conditional FILL_IN markers
